name: Personal Agent Action Router
tagline: The whiteboard architecture for a personal assistant that queues actions before tools touch the world.
thesis: The board describes a personal assistant control plane: attendant recording, AI translation, and persona context become normalized inputs; Kafka holds those inputs as queued action candidates; then a human constraint gate checks time zone, sleep, family, persona, and approval boundaries before Zapier, Replit, goal/soul/skill files, or other tools execute. The product is not just an agent. It is the action router that keeps the agent useful, observable, and socially aware.
audience: Builders of personal agents who need a real middle layer between captured intent and tool execution, especially when actions can interrupt a person, speak on their behalf, or touch external systems.
proofPoints: The left side of the board is the intake stack: attendant recording, AI translation, and persona/human context become structured inputs instead of loose notes.
The center is the router: queued action candidates move through Kafka so retries, prioritization, and audit order are explicit.
The right side is the human gate: time zone, sleep, family, and persona boundaries decide whether the action runs, waits, or asks for approval.

launchChecks.label: Whiteboard photo translated into product architecture
Bind attendant recording, translation, and persona context into one event schema
Wire Kafka action topics to Zapier, Replit, and durable goal/soul/skill tool adapters
